quoteFirst: We had not yet seen the land, but
quoteLast: we saw the Cape land bearing east at about 16 leagues distance: and, Captain Hammond being also bound to double the Cape, we jogged on together this afternoon and the next day, and had several fair sights of it; which may be seen.
title: A Voyage to New Holland, Etc. in the Year 1699
author: Dampier, William, 1651-1715

quoteLast: The enemy had one battery close by Shiloh, and another near the Hamburg road, both pouring grape and canister upon any column of troops that advanced upon the green point of water-oaks.
title: Memoirs of General W. T. Sherman, Volume I., Part 2
author: Sherman, William T. (William Tecumseh), 1820-1891

quoteFirst: The prison was heated by a hot-water system, which was shut off each day at
quoteTime: about two o’clock in the afternoon
quoteLast: , so that in the evening the atmosphere generally was very cold, so cold in fact, that frequently I would have to go to bed as early as seven o’clock, directly the cells were locked, in order to keep myself warm.
title: My Three Years in a German Prison
author: Béland, Henri, 1869-1935

quoteTime: A little after two o’clock in the afternoon
quoteLast: news of the situation was received, sent an hour or so earlier by Major-General Coke, who was then on the plateau of the slopes below Spion Kop.
title: Sir Charles Warren and Spion Kop: A Vindication
author: Defender

quoteLast: he was where he should have been still less; that is, in the same place and condition.
title: The Clarion
author: Adams, Samuel Hopkins, 1871-1958; Stevens, William Dodge, 1870-1942 [Illustrator]

quoteLast: and a savoury smell from the galley reminded us that it was about seven hours since we had breakfasted.
title: A Journal of a Tour in the Congo Free State
author: Dorman, Marcus R. P., 1868-1907

quoteFirst: Saturday morning your father and I went to the museum and saw the     statuary and paintings there, and left Jersey City
quoteLast: for     home, where we arrived at about half past eight: We had a pleasant     time, but were rather tired.
title: An Astronomer's Wife: The Biography of Angeline Hall
author: Hall, Angelo, 1868-1922

quoteTime: about two o'clock in the afternoon
quoteLast: when we set out again on our travels.
title: The Wayfarers
author: Snaith, J. C. (John Collis), 1876-1936

quoteFirst: His brigade was about to attack the Russian artillery, and
quoteLast: had hardly advanced twenty paces before the cry of “Halt!”
title: Our Cavalry
author: Rimington, Michael Frederic, 1858-1928

quoteLast: the party returned with the baggage, all well pleased that they had completed the laborious task of the portage.
title: The Journals of Lewis and Clark, 1804-1806
author: Lewis, Meriwether, 1774-1809; Clark, William, 1770-1838
